title: Neelakanteshwara temple
tagline: Neelakanteshwara temple — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Neelakanteswara temple is in Nizamabad, Telangana.
overview.description: Neelakanteswara temple is in Nizamabad, Telangana. The presiding deities of this temple are Shiva and Parvati who is also known Neelakanta. Puranas indicate that Brahma has worshipped the idol of Lord Shiva at this temple and thus this temple is one of the most important Shiva temple in India. The temple is one of the ancient temples of India with devotees thronging to this place during the Shiva Ratri festival.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad Bus Station
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad Bus Station (NZB) is a bus station located at the center of Nizamabad city in the state of Telangana, India. The bus station is owned by Telangana State Road Transport Corporation (TSRTC) and is one of the major bus stations in the newly formed state of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Gandhi Chowk
topPlaces.description: Gandhi Chowk is a 4-way traffic junction and the largest commercial and business centers in the city of Nizamabad. The place is located in the center of the city and is also the originating point of National Highway 16 which passes through the states of Telangana, Maharashtra and ends at Jagdalpur in the state of Chhattisgarh.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad, Telangana
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad, also informally known as Induru or Indur, is a city in the Indian state of Telangana. It is governed by the municipal corporation and is the headquarters of the Nizamabad district.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad mandal
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad mandal is one of the 27 mandals within Nizamabad District in the Indian state of Telangana. It is located in the revenue division of Nizamabad, while the headquarters are located in the city of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad North mandal
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad North is an administrative district located in the revenue division and city of Nizamabad under Nizamabad Urban Constituency. It is one of the 27 mandals within Nizamabad District in the Indian state of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad South mandal
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad South is an administrative division located in the revenue division and city of Nizamabad under Nizamabad Urban Constituency. It is one of the 27 mandals within Nizamabad District in the Indian state of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Ahmed Pura Colony
topPlaces.description: Ahmed Pura Colony is a locality in Nizamabad, in the Indian state of Telangana. It is located at 500 meters from the commercial center Gandhi Chowk.

topPlaces.name: Nizamabad Fort
topPlaces.description: Nizamabad Fort, also known as Nizamabad Quilla, is a fort in Nizamabad in the Indian state of Telangana. It was built in 10th century by Rashtrakuta Kings and is situated in the southwest of the city, which is 2 kilometers from Gandhi Chowk.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Neelakanteshwara temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Neelakanteshwara temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Neelakanteshwara temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Nanded Airport (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (145 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Neelakanteshwara temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Neelakanteshwara temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Neelakanteshwara temple?
faq.a: Stays in Neelakanteshwara temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Neelakanteshwara temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nizamabad Bus Station, Gandhi Chowk, Nizamabad, Telangana, Nizamabad mandal, Nizamabad North mandal.
